description_long: This is a double-bit axe featuring a broad, symmetrical steel blade with a slightly curved cutting edge on both sides. The blade is robust and has a darkened central section, likely from use or intentional design, with lighter metallic edges. The handle is made of wood, displaying a natural brown color and a visible grain pattern, and is slightly curved for ergonomic grip. The handle appears to be wrapped or textured for improved handling and control. The axe is of medium size, suitable for use in urban environments such as parks, garages, or maintenance areas. There are no visible engravings, logos, or decorative elements on the blade or handle.
description: A double-bit axe with a broad steel blade and a curved wooden handle, featuring a darkened blade center and textured grip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-edged steel blade and the curved wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blade and the handle's curvature.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the opposite side of the double-edged blade and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's thin profile and the handle's ergonomic curve.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a textured or wrapped grip, oval cross-section, ergonomic for two-handed use.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bit symmetrical steel blade with two curved cutting edges, no beard or poll, darkened central area.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
